duplicate_contradictions.reason: All three restate the gap between pathology/biomarker axis and clinical/symptom/function outcomes.
All three restate biomarker classification versus mixed clinical/pathology heterogeneity.
Both concern vascular pathology interacting with Alzheimer disease and anti-amyloid interpretation; CON-002 is the therapy-safety subtype.

split_contradictions.source_concept: Amyloid/tau centrality versus clinical effect and patient-centered endpoint uncertainty
Biomarker-defined AD versus mixed-pathology clinical heterogeneity
split_contradictions.hostile_assessment: Partly legitimate subtopics, but counted as independent contradictions without enough separation.

split_contradictions.source_concept: Vascular contribution and vascular safety constraints
split_contradictions.hostile_assessment: CON-002 is clinically distinct; CON-006 is broader and overlaps heavily.

hostile_count_basis: Merge overlapping biomarker/mixed-pathology items, merge overlapping amyloid/tau/clinical-endpoint items, merge broad vascular overlap, and exclude APOE pleiotropy plus pure causal-ordering uncertainties as independent contradictions.
